source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Commerical Distributor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life's work placed in the hands of leaders who care. Over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re actively interviewing CEO candidates for current and future acquisition opportunities. While timing varies by market and acquisition activity, selected candidates enter our CEO network and may be matched with opportunities as they become available.

For this role, we are looking for experienced operators who have led commercial distribution companies. This is an opportunity to partner with American Operator through the acquisition process, with the goal of stepping into a profitable, established business with a strong reputation, a loyal customer base, and an experienced team already in place. American Operator leads sourcing and due diligence, and the selected candidate will contribute industry insights during that process before stepping into the operating role post-close.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation set by the former owners. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a best-in-class sales force, driving customer acquisition and growth, and scaling the business to new products, customer segments and markets.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the company’s P&L with full responsibility for performance and day-to-day operations

 - Step into a newly acquired business and build relationships with suppliers, customers, and employees

 - Strategically add and cut SKUs to match customer preferences and provide upsell opportunities

 - Lead high-impact initiatives to modernize the approach to inventory management and customer relationship management

 - Travel to markets for sales rep coaching and customer relationship building

 - Foster a culture of high accountability and performance

 - Run quarterly board meetings with American Operator

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of B2B sales leadership and coaching experience

 - 4+ years of hiring and firing responsibility

 - Experience owning company P&Ls and budgets

 - MRO industrial sales background across commercial, healthcare, government, and manufacturing verticals

 - Proven sales ability to navigate C-suite decision-makers

 - Experience leading and developing a high-performing sales team in a relationship-driven business

 - Ability to travel regularly to meet with sales reps and customers

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - Sales management experience in a family-owned or private-equity-backed business

 - History of developing an organization and culture that leads to strong employee retention and tenure

 - Small business leadership experience (50 employees or less)

 - Military leadership experience

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $135,000-$175,000, plus 20% performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package (Health, vision, dental).

LOCATION

Our active markets include Delaware, Pennsylvania, Maryland, New Jersey, and Virginia. Relocation will be required if you are not currently in or near the market of the opportunity.

Ready to lead the next chapter? Before you apply, we en

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Industrial Chemicals Manufacturing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business ownership. We buy small businesses between $5M-$15M in revenue from retiring owners who want their life's work in the hands of leaders who care. We place in an experienced operator as CEO and over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re actively interviewing CEO candidates for current and future acquisition opportunities. Some candidates may be matched to businesses we already own, while others may be considered for businesses that are currently under acquisition review or expected to be acquired in the future.

While timing varies by market and acquisition activity, selected candidates enter our CEO network and may be matched with opportunities as they become available.

This will be an opportunity to step into a profitable business with a tenured team already in place. You'll assume full day-to-day operations and P&L responsibility. This is not a turnaround. Your job will be to retain the team and customers, run the business with discipline, and grow from a position of strength. American Operator is your partner from day one, with a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business you lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow the business with a focus on key performance metrics as you lead day-to-day plant operations

 - Develop and lead the production, quality, and sales team with a culture of high accountability and performance

 - Oversee production and dispensing processes with discipline on batch quality and size across a large product portfolio

 - Monitor sales, margin, and performance compared to budget to inform go-to-market strategy and pricing

 - Manage raw material procurement, plant capacity, and equipment upkeep

 - Maintain EPA product and facility registration and GHS labeling compliance across the product line

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of relevant industry experience, with at least 3 years of P&L ownership over a department or entire plant

 - Experience building high performance teams with accountability for hiring and performance improvement

 - Track record of leading successful LEAN process integrations into batch manufacturing businesses

 - Experience growing sales through a distribution network as a manufacturing rep

 - Currently located in or a willingness to relocate to one of our target markets (Texas, Florida, Illinois, Ohio, or Pennsylvania)

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - A track record of growth leading an outside sales team where you consistently exceeded targets

 - Direct experience in chemical manufacturing including, formulated products, paints and coatings, lubricants, and other products

 - Stepped in as the new leader after an ownership or leadership transition and earned the trust of the existing production and formulation team

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $130,000–$190,000, plus performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance and value creation. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package.

LOCATION

Our target acquisition markets include Texas, Florida, Illinois, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. Relocation will be required if you are not currently in or near the market of the opportunity.

Ready to lead the next chapter? Before you apply, we encourage you to visit americanoperator.com http://americanoperator.com to learn more about who we are, how we work, and what our operators have to say about the experience. When you are ready, apply today to start the conversation.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Associate
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is looking to add a highly motivated, outbound-driven Business Development Associate to join our team. This role is focused on sourcing acquisition opportunities through high-volume outreach and relationship development with intermediaries, channel partners, brokers, and referral sources. The ideal candidate thrives in a hunting environment, is energized by proactive outreach, and excels at building long-term intermediary relationships that generate consistent deal flow. This role is based in our Austin, TX office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own coverage and relationship management for a high-volume portfolio of generalist intermediaries, brokers, and channel partners, particularly those that do not publicly list deal flow.

 - Execute high-volume outbound relationship building across phone, email, LinkedIn, conferences, and referral networks to uncover proprietary and off-market acquisition opportunities.

 - Conduct ongoing follow-up, nurturing, and relationship development with intermediaries to maintain top-of-mind positioning and drive repeat deal referrals.

 - Qualify inbound and outbound opportunities and escalate promising deals to Deal Leads for deeper evaluation and review.

 - Coordinate closely with Deal Leads to ensure smooth handoff and own relationship through LOI submission..

 - Lead creation and iterative improvements of sales collateral, programming and other assets to aid in prospecting and relationship management efforts

 - Track all outreach activity, intermediary engagement, and pipeline progress within HubSpot and internal CRM systems.

 - Develop a deep understanding of intermediary landscapes, market dynamics, and sourcing channels to identify untapped relationship opportunities.

 - Participate in weekly pipeline, sourcing, and strategy meetings, providing updates on outreach performance, intermediary engagement, and market insights.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of experience in a corporate development or M&A business development role, with a focus on broker or intermediary coverage

 - Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, or related field.

 - Solid understanding of the M&A process, three-statement financials, and transaction dynamics

 - Experience using HubSpot, CRM systems, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Slack, Zoom, Google Workspace, and related productivity tools.

 - Proven success building out channel or referral partnerships (e.g., indirect sales)

 - Strong ability to build rapport quickly and maintain long-term professional relationships across a broad network of intermediaries and referral partners.

 - Highly motivated self-starter with a hunter mentality, comfort operating in a metrics-driven environment, and happy to hop on a plane

 - Exceptional verbal and written communication skills with strong follow-up discipline.

 - Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple relationship pipelines simultaneously.

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with investment, deal, and operations teams.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.






ABOUT THE TEAM

American Operator exists because we believe the American Dream is worth protecting, and that small businesses are the backbone of our communities. To do this well, we need a team that reflects the real world: operators, builders, investors, and problem-solvers from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences.




MISSION-DRIVEN

Every company claims to be mission-driven, but our mission is simple, tangible, and urgent: preserve the legacy of America’s small businesses and create more pathways to meaningful ownership.

We partner with operators who want to step into leadership, take responsibility for great companies, and continue the work that founders spent decades building. When we succeed, communities keep their employers, families keep their livelihoods, and operators get the chance to build wealth through real ownership.

We work har

source_jobs.description:

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life's work placed in the hands of leaders who care. While timing varies by market and acquisition activity, selected candidates enter our CEO network and may be matched with opportunities as they become available.

This will be an opportunity to step into a profitable, established landscaping business with a strong reputation, loyal customer base, and an experienced team already in place.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations, team leadership, and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a great team, driving customer acquisition and growth, executing with operational discipline, and scaling the business from a position of strength.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow the business with a focus on key performance metrics as you lead all business day-to-day operations

 - Oversee the estimating and bidding process to make sure that the business is winning projects profitably

 - Develop and lead crew leaders and office staff with a culture of high accountability and performance

 - Ensure crews deliver consistent, high quality service across every account and route

 - Protect and grow the recurring maintenance base through strong account retention and renewals

 - Recruit, manage, and develop field teams in a physically challenging industry with seasonality

 - Win new accounts and expand existing ones through enhancements, local relationships, and disciplined estimating

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 10+ years of leadership experience with at least 3 years of direct field crew oversight

 - Experience owning P&L performance

 - Track record of leading and growing a recurring-contract, field-services business with crews and seasonal labor (landscaping, grounds maintenance, janitorial or facility services, snow, pest control, or a similar trade)

 - Currently located in or a willingness to relocate to one of our target markets (North Carolina, South Carolina, Delaware, Arizona, or Tennessee)

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - A competitive spirit that always finds a way to win

 - Military leadership experience

 - Experience managing hardscaping or other residential or commercial construction projects

 - An Irrigator license or are a Certified Commercial Pesticide Applicator

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $130,000–$190,000, plus performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance and value creation. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package.

LOCATION

Our active markets include North Carolina, South Carolina, Delaware, Arizona, and Tennessee. Relocation will be required if you are not currently in or near the market of the opportunity.

Ready to lead the next chapter? Before you apply, we encourage you to visit americanoperator.com http://americanoperator.com to learn

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Residential Plumbing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life's work placed in the hands of leaders who care. Over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re actively interviewing CEO candidates for current and future acquisition opportunities. Some candidates may be matched to businesses we already own, while others may be considered for businesses that are currently under acquisition review or expected to be acquired in the future.

While timing varies by market and acquisition activity, selected candidates enter our CEO network and may be matched with opportunities as they become available.

This will be an opportunity to step into a profitable, established plumbing business with a strong reputation, loyal customer base, and an experienced team already in place.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations, team leadership, and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a great team, driving customer acquisition and growth, executing with operational discipline, and scaling the business from a position of strength.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead day-to-day operations and drive growth across key business performance metrics

 - Build and Develop a high-performing and accountable team

 - Oversee field operations to deliver exceptional customer service and quality of work

 - Grow the direct-to-homeowner customer base by managing the funnel from sourcing leads through job completion

 - Oversee warehouse operations, manage inventory levels to support field operations

 - Recruit, develop, and retain top plumbing talent

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy and execution

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - Successful track record of operating and growing a residential plumbing business

 - Experience owning P&L performance, including budgets and KPI targets

 - Experience building, leading, and holding high-performing teams accountable, including responsibility for hiring and talent development

 - Deep understanding of residential plumbing operations, with the ability to evaluate field service quality across water heater, water treatment systems, drain cleaning, sewer and line replacement, and related services.

 - Hold a master plumbing license or have the ability to obtain one within 12 months

 - Currently based in, or willing to relocate to, one of the following markets: Texas, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, Colorado, or Tennessee

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Track record of building and strengthening a customer-first culture centered on trust, service quality, and operational excellence

 - Experience successfully leading a business through a leadership transition and building trust with the existing team

 - Built or managed recurring revenue programs through membership plans in a plumbing or home services business

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $130,000–$190,000, plus performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance and value creation. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package.

LOCATION

Our target acquisition markets include Texas, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, Colorado, and Tennessee. Relocation will be required if you are not currently in or near the market of the opportunity.

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is seeking an entrepreneurial and highly tactical Controller to build and scale our accounting infrastructure. American Operator is a Holding Company that owns a rapidly growing portfolio of SMBs with a focus on industrial and services businesses. The Controller will play a critical role in our ability to grow our portfolio through acquisitions and manage existing Portfolio Companies, including owning the accounting function, providing the financial insights and reports needed for operating portfolio management, and assisting in capital raises.



A breakout candidate will drive the design and implementation of a best-in-class accounting function that quickly integrates new acquisitions, streamlines cascading closes of the books, manages HoldCo consolidation, provides actionable business insights to existing companies, and can scale an order of magnitude. This role will work cross-functionally, from pre-close integration to portfolio performance and finance. This role will report directly to our CFO.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and build a standardized accounting infrastructure from the ground up, creating consistent processes, controls, reporting structures, and workflows across the portfolio. Bias towards modern tooling and AI-enablement.

 - Lead accounting integration efforts for newly acquired businesses, including opening balance sheet preparation, treatment of goodwill, chart of accounts mapping, reporting standardization, and system migrations.

 - Own the monthly, quarterly, and annual close process across a growing portfolio of operating companies, including GL processes, reconciliations and financial statements.

 - Prepare and deliver consolidated financial statements, lender reporting packages, executive dashboards, and board-level financial materials.

 - Manage resourcing needed to action portfolio and holding company accounting needs, including external accounting firms, auditors, tax advisors, and compliance partners to ensure accurate reporting, audit readiness, and alignment with company accounting standards.

 - Support tax compliance across multiple entity structures, including LLCs, S-Corps, and pass-through entities operating across multiple states.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of progressive accounting experience, including 3+ years in a Controller, Assistant Controller, Director of Accounting, or similar leadership roles.

 - Significant experience in a multi-entity environment with responsibility for consolidated financial reporting and intercompany accounting, ideally in a roll-up or holding company environment.

 - Experience supporting acquisitive business models with an understanding of purchase price allocation, tax optimization, and opening balance sheets.

 - Demonstrated success implementing or improving accounting systems, including ERP migrations, standardized chart of accounts, and workflow tooling.

 - Experience leveraging a variety of stakeholders, including external accounting firms, offshore bookkeepers, auditors, and tax advisors.

 - Ability to translate financial information into practical business insights for operators and non-finance stakeholders.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in home or business services (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, pest control, restoration) or industrials (light manufacturing, distribution)

 - CPA preferred but not required.

 - Past exposure to highly acquisitive environments with multiple M&A events.

 - Interest in leveraging AI-enabled tools to speed automation and increase accuracy.

 - Experience implementing an ERP platform across multiple operating companies.

 - Experience transitioning accounting functions from external accounting firms to an internal finance organization.

 - Experience supporting businesses with recurring lender reporting and covenant monitoring responsibilities.




COMPENSATION

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a disc

source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is looking to add a Revenue Operations Manager to the team. We are looking for someone to own the systems and playbooks that power our business development team. This role will be responsible for CRM architecture & optimization, GTM tool selection and integration, lifecycle marketing, and reporting.

Your work will enable our team to maximize the number of qualified small business owners we’re in discussions with, deliver ongoing value through lifecycle marketing, and earn the right to be their succession plan of choice. This role will report directly to the CEO.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as operational partner to Business Development leadership, working closely to drive metrics and enable teammates.

 - Own top-of-funnel conversion from lead to qualified target, delivering a consistent, high quality pipeline of direct and referred business owners interested in exiting to American Operator.

 - Design and implement Hubspot improvements for marketing and sales, including pipeline & stage definitions, attribution, workflows, automations, reporting and data hygiene.

 - Build individual and team-level reports and dashboards, surfacing KPI performance and trends.

 - Lead the selection, integration and onboarding for GTM tools to drive higher conversion, volume or quality.

 - Own lifecycle marketing to referral partners and current owners, staying top of mind and nurturing folks to partner with American Operator.

 - Maintain and improve the integration layer between our GTM tools and CRM, ensuring data is clean and systems in sync.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years in a dedicated Sales Operations or Revenue Operations role, with a track record of supporting high-velocity outbound sales.

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Information Systems, or a related field preferred.

 - Deep hands-on experience with the modern GTM stack: Clay, Apollo, HubSpot or Salesforce, Outreach or Instantly, etc.

 - Strong command of Hubspot, including workflows, automations, custom objects, reporting and integrations.

 - You’ve led lifecycle and email marketing initiatives to boost pipeline influenced rates and ultimately drive higher conversions.

 - You think in funnels and measure everything - open rates, reply rates, meeting conversion, and cost per opportunity are metrics you track obsessively

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
   
   





PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Past experience in at a sales-driven company serving the SMB market

 - Working knowledge of financial concepts, M&A expertise being a bonus

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Investments
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is seeking a Vice President of Investments to lead the end-to-end acquisition of small- to medium-sized businesses, shaping American Operator’s portfolio for long-term growth and operational excellence. This is a senior individual contributor role where you will own deal execution, drive high-quality investment decisions, and serve as a key partner to leadership across the investment lifecycle.

You will run-point on small business acquisitions from LOI to close, managing diligence, negotiations, and underwriting. You will be the primary point-of-contact for the retiring owners we partner with as well as the operators we back, requiring strong relationship management and communication skills.

The ideal candidate for this role has a strong mix of EQ and IQ, are familiar with small business owners, and have experience with closing acquisitions of Main Street- or Lower Middle Market deals.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the deal lifecycle from offer through close, with accountability for deal quality, pacing, and outcomes.

 - Evaluate and prioritize investment opportunities, forming clear perspectives and recommendations on where to deploy capital.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for business owners, building trust and navigating complex, high-emotion transactions.

 - Lead structuring, negotiation, and execution of transactions, ensuring alignment with firm strategy and risk-return objectives.

 - Partner with operators and leadership on post-close integration and value creation initiatives.

 - Partner with leadership on sourcing strategy and pipeline development, helping maintain a consistent flow of high-quality opportunities.

 - Work closely with internal stakeholders and external advisors throughout diligence and execution.

 - Represent the firm externally with intermediaries, advisors, and within the SMB ecosystem.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of relevant experience in private equity and/or investment banking.

 - Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, or related field.

 - Demonstrated experience owning transaction execution from sourcing through close.

 - Deep expertise in financial modeling, structuring, and negotiations.

 - Diverse industry experience, with a preference for distribution, manufacturing or services businesses.

 - Strong investment judgment and ability to evaluate businesses holistically, including operational and strategic considerations.

 - Proven ability to manage multiple transactions simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence internal and external stakeholders.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - MBA from a top-tier program.

 - Experience working with or investing in small and medium-sized businesses.

 - Personal connection to small businesses or passion for preserving the American Dream.

 - Experience with lower middle market / SMB acquisitions.




COMPENSATION

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus and equity. Please note that compensation is determined based on a variety of factors, including, but not limited to, relevant work experience, education, skills, and other qualifications specific to the role.

$150K - $200K




ABOUT THE TEAM

American Operator exists because we believe the American Dream is worth protecting, and that small businesses are the backbone of our communities. To do this well, we need a team that reflects the real world: operators, builders, investors, and problem-solvers from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences.




MISSION-DRIVEN

Every company claims to be mission-driven, but our mission is simple, tangible, and urgent: preserve the legacy of America’s small businesses and create more pathways to meaningful ownership.

We partner with operators who want to step into leadership, take responsibility for great companies, and continu

source_jobs.title: Deal Sourcing Associate
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is seeking a driven, high-energy Deal Sourcing Associate to generate proprietary acquisition opportunities. This role sits at the very start of the deal process, focused on building relationships with business owners and sourcing off-market opportunities. If you thrive in high-volume, results-driven environments, enjoy outbound prospecting, and want to build a career in private equity, investing, or M&A, this role is for you. This role is based at our Austin, TX office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Proactively research and reach out to target business owners via cold calls, emails, LinkedIn, SMS, and other channels to generate off-market deal flow.

 - Lead early conversations with business owners, clearly explaining who we are, the types of businesses we partner with, and why owners choose to work with us.

 - Conduct initial discovery meetings to gather baseline information, evaluate fit, and determine next steps for each opportunity.

 - Maintain and update our CRM with all outreach activity, prospect information, and follow-ups to keep deals moving forward.

 - Work closely with the Investments team to hand off qualified opportunities and continue nurturing owner relationships.

 - Consistently follow up with prospects over time, building trust and rapport to maintain a healthy pipeline.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1+ years of experience in sales, business development, or deal sourcing (cold calling experience highly preferred).

 - Comfortable and confident making high-volume outbound calls.

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills.

 - Self-starter with the ability to manage multiple prospects simultaneously.

 - Experience with CRMs (Hubspot or similar) and tracking outreach activity.

 - Interest in private equity, investing, or M&A, and willingness to learn financial basics.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Undergraduate degree in business, finance, or related field.

 - Exposure to small business operations (family business, internships, etc.).

 - Familiarity with basic financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, tax returns) is a plus.



Compensation: This role offers substantial performance-based upside. We pay 50 bps per acquisition, which equates to ~$25,000 (per closed deal). At 6-12 deals per year, that equates to $150,000–$300,000+ in total compensation for strong performers. Bonus payouts are tied to individual performance metrics and closed transactions and are subject to plan terms and company performance.






ABOUT THE TEAM

American Operator exists because we believe the American Dream is worth protecting, and that small businesses are the backbone of our communities. To do this well, we need a team that reflects the real world: operators, builders, investors, and problem-solvers from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences.

We know that diversity of thought, discipline, and lived experience leads to better decisions and stronger businesses. We’re intentional about bringing together people who care deeply about ownership, stewardship, and keeping great companies in capable hands. Our work depends on collaboration, humility, and a shared belief in the value of Main Street.

We’re a team shaped by different industries, hometowns, and career paths, and we believe those differences are exactly what prepare us to serve small-business owners and future CEOs across the country.




MISSION-DRIVEN

Every company claims to be mission-driven, but our mission is simple, tangible, and urgent: preserve the legacy of America’s small businesses and create more pathways to meaningful ownership.

We partner with operators who want to step into leadership, take responsibility for great companies, and continue the work that founders spent decades building. When we succeed, communities keep their employers, families keep their livelihoods, and operators get the chance to build wealth through real ownership.

We work hard because we know what’s at stake: generational businesses that de

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Residential Roofing Contractor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life's work placed in the hands of leaders who care. Over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for an experienced operator to lead a residential roofing business as CEO in partnership with American Operator. This is a rare opportunity to step into a profitable, established business with a strong reputation, referral network, and an experienced team already in place.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations, team leadership, and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a great team, driving customer acquisition and growth, executing with operational discipline, and scaling the business from a position of strength.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the company’s P&L with full responsibility for performance and day-to-day operations

 - Step into a newly acquired business and build relationships with suppliers, customers, and employees

 - Oversee and participate in the estimating and bidding process to ensure that the business is winning projects profitably

 - Manage and lead the sales, production, service, and office staff with accountability

 - Ensure crews deliver consistent, high quality service to every customer

 - Win new accounts and expand existing ones through enhancements, local relationships, and disciplined estimating

 - Run quarterly board meetings with American Operator

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of leadership experience with at least 2 years in residential or commercial roofing field work

 - Track record of leading and growing a home services business in a competitive industry

 - Strong P&L management skills with experience managing insurance billing, collections, and cash flow

 - Managed a subcontracted labor crew with reliable & high-quality production outcomes

 - Built an effective outbound sales team in the roofing industry

 - Experience estimating commercial and/or residential roofing systems

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - Currently hold or can immediately obtain a roofing license in a local Colorado municipality

 - Experience working with insurance adjusters as a construction contractor

 - Military leadership experience

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $150,000, plus 20% performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package (Health, vision, dental).

LOCATION

This business is located in Colorado's Front Range. The exact location of this business will be revealed during the interview process.

Ready to lead the next chapter? Before you apply, we encourage you to visit americanoperator.com http://americanoperator.com to learn more about who we are, how we work, and what our operators have to say about the experience. When you are ready, apply today to start the conversation..

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO Program
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership & ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life’s work placed in capable, values-driven hands. Our mission is to ensure great businesses stay local, stay strong, and stay in the hands of leaders who care.

Operators who partner with us step into the CEO role of an established company and receive the support, coaching, and community needed to grow it for the next generation. Over time, they follow a clear pathway toward becoming a significant equity owner if growth targets are met.




IS THIS YOU?

 - Are you a proven industry pro who’s ready to build a business for yourself?

 - Do you want to lead a business end-to-end instead of managing one slice of it?

 - Have you dreamed of running your own business but haven’t had the idea or chance?

 - Do you care deeply about people, service, and building organizations that last?

 - Would you relocate for the right long-term opportunity to build your future?




THE OPPORTUNITY

By joining our Portfolio CEO Program, you position yourself for future CEO opportunities in an established small business where strong operational leadership is needed most. When an opportunity aligns with your capabilities, we’ll reach out and you’ll work directly with our team to understand the business, align on priorities, and prepare for a successful leadership transition.

As the Portfolio CEO, you’ll run the business with full accountability, driving performance, developing the team, and ensuring stability during and after the handoff. Throughout the journey, American Operator provides leadership transition support, strategic guidance, and a network of peers so you can focus on delivering results and building a long-term path toward ownership.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead day-to-day operations and own 100% of the company’s P&L.

 - Manage and develop the existing team to achieve strong execution and accountability.

 - Oversee customer relationships, quality control, and job profitability.

 - Maintain vendor and supplier relationships.

 - Analyze operational data to make informed decisions about pricing, staffing, and capital allocation.

 - Create and execute annual budgets, forecasts, and growth plans.

 - Partner with American Operator and key stakeholders to drive the company’s next stage of success.

 - Build credibility with the existing team, customers, and owner during the transition period.

 - Maintain operational discipline and uphold the company’s legacy and reputation.

 - Deliver measurable improvement in revenue, profitability, and cash flow.

 - Ensure safe, compliant, and efficient operations.

 - Take full ownership of business performance and culture.

 - Demonstrate leadership grounded in accountability, humility, and sound judgment.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years of operational leadership experience in an American Operator target industry. (Preferably Manufacturing, Value-Add Distribution, Flooring, Electrical or Plumbing Services).

 - 5+ years of hiring/firing authority.

 - 5+ years of responsibility over P&L and/or company budgets.

 - Proven success managing teams and complex operations.

 - Small business leadership experience.

 - Excellent communication and relationship management skills.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with a long-term approach to business building.

 - Must be willing to regionally locate (5-state area)






Compensation: Base salary range of $150,000-$180,000, along with a comprehensive benefits package that may include health coverage, paid time off, and holidays. The role is eligible for additional compensation, including equity participation; the amount varies based on the portfolio company and deal structure.



*Location Disclaimer: Opportunities to lead an American Operator acquisition business are based in various locations across the US and relocation will be required f

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Portfolio Performance
source_jobs.description:
American Operator is looking to add VP of Portfolio Performance to partner directly with the businesses in our portfolio. This role combines strategic thinking with hands-on execution and relationship management. You will build up a book of ~10 businesses and partner with them to hit our revenue and earnings targets, while building healthy cultures, operational excellence, and clear visibility. You’ll work closely with both the investment team and company leadership, acting as a trusted advisor and execution partner.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the primary point of contact between HQ and portfolio company leadership.

 - Collaborate with the investment team to validate and implement value creation strategies.

 - Identify and execute high-impact initiatives across functions (e.g., pricing, sales, operations, org design).

 - Drive performance against operating plans, proactively identifying risks and solving issues.

 - Build strong relationships with operators and influence outcomes without direct authority.

 - Support weekly, monthly, and quarterly reporting and board communications.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with internal teams (investments, integrations, platform).

 - Travel regularly (50–75%) to work directly with portfolio companies.



MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of experience in management consulting, private equity, or similar roles.

 - Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business Administration, or related field.

 - Background across multiple industries and business models.

 - Strong financial acumen (e.g., working capital, IRR, MOIC, three-statement modeling).

 - Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems and execute across multiple business functions.

 - Proven ability to influence and build trust with diverse stakeholders, you are a force multiplier and can drive buy-in.

 - High adaptability and ability to quickly ramp in unfamiliar environments.

 - Willingness to travel extensively and work on-site with portfolio companies.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience working with small or medium-sized businesses.

 - Experience driving growth initiatives in B2B and/or B2C settings.

 - Blend of consulting and hands-on operating experience.




COMPENSATION

The anticipated base salary range for this position is listed below. Total compensation may also include a discretionary performance-based bonus and equity. Please note that compensation is determined based on a variety of factors, including, but not limited to, relevant work experience, education, skills, and other qualifications specific to the role.

$150K - $200K




ABOUT THE TEAM

American Operator exists because we believe the American Dream is worth protecting, and that small businesses are the backbone of our communities. To do this well, we need a team that reflects the real world: operators, builders, investors, and problem-solvers from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences.




MISSION-DRIVEN

Every company claims to be mission-driven, but our mission is simple, tangible, and urgent: preserve the legacy of America’s small businesses and create more pathways to meaningful ownership.

We partner with operators who want to step into leadership, take responsibility for great companies, and continue the work that founders spent decades building. When we succeed, communities keep their employers, families keep their livelihoods, and operators get the chance to build wealth through real ownership.

We work hard because we know what’s at stake: generational businesses that deserve to thrive, and talented operators who deserve the chance to lead them. And while we measure our success in deals closed and businesses grown, the real reward is seeing these companies continue to serve their people and their towns, stronger than before.

Where else do you get the opportunity to help someone become a CEO, preserve a founder’s life’s work, and strengthen a community, all in the same job? That’s what makes our work both serious and

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Powder Coating Shop
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We buy small businesses between $5M-$15M in revenue from retiring owners who want their life's work in the hands of leaders who care. We place in an experienced operator as CEO and over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re actively interviewing CEO candidates for current and future acquisition opportunities. Some candidates may be matched to businesses we already own, while others may be considered for businesses that are currently under acquisition review or expected to be acquired in the future.

While timing varies by market and acquisition activity, selected candidates enter our CEO network and may be matched with opportunities as they become available.

This will be an opportunity to step into a profitable, established Powder Coating business with a strong reputation, loyal customer base, and an experienced team already in place.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations, team leadership, and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a great team, driving customer acquisition and growth, executing with operational discipline, and scaling the business from a position of strength.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow the business with a focus on key performance metrics as you lead day-to-day plant operations

 - Develop and lead the production team, including line leads and applicators, with a culture of high accountability and performance

 - Oversee production on the finishing line (pretreatment, booths, and cure ovens) with discipline on throughput, quality, and on-time delivery

 - Build upon existing customer relationships and grow into new end markets to reduce industry concentration

 - Own company compliance for hazardous waste, and other regulations

 - Manage equipment upkeep and capex

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of relevant industry experience, with at least 3 years of P&L ownership over a department or entire plant

 - Experience building high performance team hourly team with accountability for hiring and performance improvement

 - You have led operations with a focus on batch production runs on tight timelines

 - Track record of profitably growing sales for job shops that rely on reputation and service quality

 - Currently located in or a willingness to relocate to one of our target markets (Michigan, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Texas, Indiana, Illinois, or Wisconsin)

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - Direct experience within powder coating, liquid paint, metal finishing, and metal fabrication.

 - Owned responsibility for the compliance and record-keeping that comes with a permitted facility (air permits, VOC logs, hazardous waste)

 - Diversified a customer base across end markets to reduce concentration, and you bring direct powder coating, liquid paint, or metal-finishing experience as a strong plus

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $130,000–$190,000, plus performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance and value creation. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package.

LOCATION

Our target acquisition markets include Michigan, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Texas, Indiana, Illinois, and Wisconsin. Relocation will be required if you are not currently in or n

 - Win and retain customers through service quality, local reputation, and disciplined pricing

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years of leadership experience

 - Hold an active master electrician license with a strong understanding of residential electrical codes and requirements

 - Ability to audit your teams work on panel upgrades, service replacements, circuit installations, surge protection, generator installations, EV charger installations, and lighting upgrades

 - Experience building high performance teams with accountability for hiring and performance improvement

 - Currently located in or a willingness to relocate to one of our target markets (Texas, Georgia, North Carolina, Colorado, or Tennessee)

WHAT SETS YOU APART

source_jobs.title: Portfolio CEO: Tent & Event Rental Company
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AMERICAN OPERATOR

American Operator exists to keep the American Dream alive through small-business leadership and ownership. We place experienced operators into businesses of retiring owners who want their life's work placed in the hands of leaders who care. Over time, our CEOs follow a clear pathway to earn significant equity in the business they lead.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for an experienced operator to lead a tent & event supplies rental company as CEO in partnership with American Operator. This is a rare opportunity to step into a profitable, established business with a strong reputation, loyal customer base, and an experienced team already in place.

As CEO, you'll assume full responsibility for day-to-day operations, team leadership, and P&L performance. This is not a turnaround situation, it's an opportunity to build on a solid foundation. Your focus will be on developing and retaining a great team, driving customer acquisition and growth, executing with operational discipline, and scaling the business from a position of strength.

American Operator will be your partner from day one, providing strategic support, resources, and guidance while empowering you to lead independently. Most importantly, you'll have a clear path to earning significant equity ownership in the business you grow and lead.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow the business with a focus on key performance metrics as you lead day-to-day operations

 - Develop and lead sales, field crew leaders, and office staff with a culture of high accountability and performance

 - Coach and build an outbound sales team focused on corporate relationships and referral partners

 - Manage the budget and capital decisions associated with a relatively asset-intensive business

 - Adapt the business's sales, marketing, and inventory based on the latest trends in weddings, corporate events, graduations, etc.

 - Recruit, manage, and develop field teams in a physically challenging industry with seasonality

 - Partner with American Operator on strategy, budgeting, and growth planning

REQUIREMENTS

 - 10+ years of leadership experience with direct experience as a sales and operations leader

 - Experience building high performance teams with accountability for hiring and performance improvement

 - Track record of leading and growing an organization that's driven by local relationships and referral networks

WHAT SETS YOU APART

 - A competitive spirit that always finds a way to win

 - Experience as a sales or operations leader in the catering, event planning, or rental industry

 - Stepped in as the new leader after an ownership or leadership transition and earned the trust of a long-tenured team

 - Military leadership experience

COMPENSATION

Base salary: $150,000, plus performance-based bonus opportunities, distributions, and meaningful equity participation beginning at 10% ownership, with the potential to increase based on performance and value creation. Total compensation varies by portfolio company, individual performance, and deal structure, and includes a comprehensive benefits package.

LOCATION

Ready to lead the next chapter? Before you apply, we encourage you to visit americanoperator.com http://americanoperator.com to learn more about who we are, how we work, and what our operators have to say about the experience. When you are ready, apply today to start the conversation.

notes: American Operator is a holding company that acquires and operates small businesses, with a focus on industrial and services businesses.
